1. Demolition and Preparation,,,,

1.2 Debris Removal,2000,,,
2. Structural Work,,,,

Subtotal,=SUM(B2:B21),=SUM(C2:C21),=C22-B22,
Contingency (10%),=B22*0.1,=C22*0.1,=C23-B23,
Total Project Cost,=SUM(B22:B23),=SUM(C22:C23),=C24-B24,

Here is the data in a spreadsheet format:

```csv
Category,Estimated Cost,Actual Cost,Variance,Notes
Demolition and Preparation,,,,
Demolition,5000,,,
Debris Removal,2000,,,
Structural Work,,,,
Floor Leveling,8000,,,
Structural Reinforcement,7000,,,
Systems Installation,,,,
Electrical,8000,,,
Plumbing,7000,,,
HVAC,10000,,,
Kitchen Installation,,,,
Cabinetry,12000,,,
Countertops,6000,,,
Appliances,7000,,,
Room Construction,,,,
Framing,5000,,,
Drywall,4000,,,
Painting,3000,,,
Flooring,12000,,,
Main Living Area Finishes,8000,,,
Labor,40000,,,
Permits and Inspections,3000,,,
Subtotal,=SUM(B2:B21),=SUM(C2:C21),=C22-B22,
Contingency (10%),=B22*0.1,=C22*0.1,=C23-B23,
Total Project Cost,=SUM(B22:B23),=SUM(C22:C23),=C24-B24,
```

You can copy this CSV data into a spreadsheet application like Microsoft Excel or Google Sheets. The formulas should automatically calculate the subtotal, contingency amount, and total project cost once you enter the actual costs.
